How to Create a DJ Mix Tracklist (The Right Way)

March 12, 2025•6 min read

Introduction

A tracklist is the written record of every song in a DJ mix — title, artist, and ideally the timestamp where each track starts. It's how listeners find the songs they love, how you get credit for your curation, and how rights holders track usage of their music. A well-formatted tracklist is one of the most important parts of sharing a mix online.

Why Tracklists Matter

For listeners: When a song makes someone feel something at the 47-minute mark of your mix, they want to know what it is. A tracklist with timestamps lets them find it instantly.

For discoverability: YouTube, Mixcloud, and Beatport each index tracklist content for search. A mix tagged with "Floating Points", "Four Tet", and "Burial" will surface in searches for those artists.

For rights management: Most streaming platforms use tracklist data to process royalty payments to rights holders. Providing accurate information is both ethical and helps keep your mix online.

For your own archive: After years of DJing, your old tracklists become a valuable record of your development as a selector.

Method 1: Track Manually During the Mix

The most accurate approach. Keep a notepad (physical or digital) at your side while mixing and write down each track as you play it. Include the timestamp when you drop each track.

Tips:

  • Use a timer app on your phone that you start at the beginning of the recording
  • Write the track name as you cue it up, not after you've mixed it in — it's easy to forget in the moment
  • Note any edits, loops, or acapellas you used

Method 2: Use Audio Fingerprinting (Automated)

For live sets where you were too focused on the mix to take notes, or for old recordings where you've forgotten the tracklist, audio fingerprinting is the fastest solution.

45 Mix Trackr automates this entirely:

1. Upload your mix file (MP3, WAV, MP4, M4A up to 500MB)

2. The tool splits it into segments and sends each to ACRCloud's fingerprinting database

3. Within minutes, you get a complete tracklist with song titles, artists, album covers, and timestamps

4. Download the SRT subtitle file — timestamps are already formatted correctly for YouTube

Formatting Your Tracklist

YouTube format (with chapters):

Tracklist:

0:00 Artist Name - Track Title

4:23 Artist Name - Track Title

8:47 Artist Name - Track Title

YouTube converts these into clickable chapter markers in the video timeline.

Mixcloud format:

Mixcloud has a built-in tracklist editor. Enter each track and it cross-references against its rights database automatically.

Instagram caption format:

Space is limited — list 5–10 highlights with emojis for visual appeal:

🎵 Artist - Track Title

🎵 Artist - Track Title

Full tracklist in bio link

What to Do When You Can't Identify a Track

Some tracks — especially dubplates, acetates, unreleased tracks, or very obscure pressings — won't be in any fingerprinting database. Options:

1. Search SoundHound or Shazam manually on that segment

2. Post a clip to Reddit's r/ifyoulikeblank or music identification communities

3. Contact the DJ directly — many DJs are happy to share their tracklists if asked politely

4. Label it as "Unknown" rather than leaving it blank — it's better to be honest

Timestamping Tips

  • Start timing from the first audible beat, not from the beginning of the file (which may have silence)
  • For blended transitions, use the moment when the new track becomes the dominant element
  • Round to the nearest second — precision to milliseconds isn't necessary

Archiving Your Tracklists

Store your tracklists somewhere accessible — a simple Google Sheets document works well. Columns:

  • Mix title
  • Date
  • Venue/event
  • Full tracklist with timestamps

Over years of DJing, this archive becomes invaluable — for booking materials, retrospective mixes, and personal reference.

Conclusion

A complete, well-formatted tracklist is a mark of professionalism and respect for your audience. Whether you track manually, use audio fingerprinting, or combine both methods, the effort pays off in listener engagement, discoverability, and your own archival record. Start the habit with every mix you record.

Identify your DJ mix instantly

Upload any audio or video mix and get a full tracklist with song titles, artists, and album covers in minutes.
